start.speaker: Jailer Ordo
start.text: Off the boat from Khorinis and straight into my yard. You know what the last man who claimed to carry 'urgent news for the King' got? A shorter rope than he asked for. So. The nameless prisoner speaks — or hangs at dawn with the other liars.

options.text: Grab the bars between you. Bend them. Slowly. While holding his gaze.

effects.text: The iron groaned. Ordo went pale and listened to every word about the orc fleet.

options.text: Mention that you counted the keys on his ring. And which one is missing.

effects.text: A jailer who loses a key doesn't report it. He makes arrangements. Ordo is now an arrangement.

options.text: Describe the orc war-banners you saw at sea — clan by clan, in the old tongue.

effects.text: No escaped convict knows the banner-speech of the clans. Ordo stopped smiling halfway through the second name.

options.text: Ask him how many men he's hanged who told the truth.

effects.text: Ordo didn't answer. But he stopped reaching for the rope, and that was an answer.

warning.speaker: Jailer Ordo
warning.text: ...An orc fleet. Off the coast. If you're lying, you hang. If you're not — gods — then this prison is the safest place in Myrtana and I'm not fool enough to say that twice. The rebels in the Highlands pay for news like yours. I never said that. Now get back to your cell before the watch changes.

options.text: [Return to the shadows of the yard]

effects.text: The rebels in the Highlands. A jailer's slip, or a jailer's mercy. Either way: a way out.
